title: Monsoon Mushrooms Slovakian Dumplings
description: Pungent and crispy Slovakian dish featuring double cream and braised mushrooms, wrapped in corn husks and suitable for a shojin-friendly diet. This monsoon-inspired recipe combines earthy flavors with a touch of creaminess.
instructions: Soak the corn husks in water for 30 minutes to make them pliable.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the mushrooms, caraway seeds, paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet and cook until the mushrooms release their liquid and start to brown.
Stir in the double cream and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and braise the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
To assemble the dumplings, lay a corn husk flat and place a tablespoon of the mushroom mixture in the center.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the filling and roll the dumpling up, repeating with the remaining corn husks and filling.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with additional caraway seeds and paprika if desired.
